title: Injection vulnerabilities - SonarQube for IDE Docs - IntelliJ
content:
# Injection vulnerabilities

## [Prerequisites](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-for-ide/intellij/using/taint-vulnerabilities/#prerequisites "Prerequisites")

- Running SonarQube for IDE in connected mode with SonarQube Cloud or a SonarQube Server commercial edition is required. See the pages on [Connected mode](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-for-ide/intellij/team-features/connected-mode/ "Connected mode") and [Connected mode setup](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-for-ide/intellij/team-features/connected-mode-setup/ "Connected mode setup") for more details.
- Your project should be analyzed regularly, ideally by your CI server.

### [Limitations](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-for-ide/intellij/using/taint-vulnerabilities/#limitations "Limitations")

- Only taint vulnerabilities in open files are shown in the IDE.
- SonarQube for IDE does not support short-lived branches therefore, when running in connected mode with SonarQube Cloud, you must work with [long-lived branches](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-cloud/enriching/branch-analysis-setup/#long-lived-and-short-lived-branches "long-lived branches"); SonarQube Server does not distinguish between long- and short-lived branches. 

  - SonarQube for IntelliJ follows [these methods to choose which branch](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-for-ide/intellij/team-features/connected-mode/#branch-awareness "these methods to choose which branch") (in SonarQube (Server, Cloud)) to sync with its local analysis, including the synchronization of taint vulnerabilities in your IDE.
- You’re limited by your SonarQube (Server, Cloud) plan restrictions regarding lines of code limits and branches you can analyze.

## [How to display injection vulnerabilities](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-for-ide/intellij/using/taint-vulnerabilities/#how-to-display-taint-vulnerabilities "How to display injection vulnerabilities")

1. Set up [Connected mode](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-for-ide/intellij/team-features/connected-mode-setup/ "Connected mode") and bind your project to SonarQube (Server, Cloud).

2. Open the SonarQube for IDE tool window and select the **Taint Vulnerabilities** tab.

3. The tab should display the list of taint vulnerabilities that are present on open files.

## [How to fix your taint vulnerabilities](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-for-ide/intellij/using/taint-vulnerabilities/#how-to-fix-your-taint-issues "How to fix your taint vulnerabilities")

Because the detection of taint vulnerabilities requires that you run in connected mode, any changes you make to the code must be analyzed by your instance of SonarQube (Server, Cloud). Here are two options to resolve taint vulnerabilities displayed by SonarQube for IDE:

- After you fix the taint vulnerability in your IDE, commit your code to the server and rerun the analysis on SonarQube (Server, Cloud). The new status (of the vulnerability) will be reflected in your IDE.
- Go to the issue in SonarQube (Server, Cloud) and mark it as **Accept** or **False positive**. The new status will be updated locally in less than one minute.

When running in connected mode with SonarQube Server 10.4 or newer, **Won’t Fix** becomes **Accept**.

Some **Taint Vulnerabilities** are eligible for getting AI-generated fix suggestions in the IDE. Please check the requirements for using [AI CodeFix in your IDE](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-for-ide/intellij/ai-capabilities/ai-codefix/#ai-codefix "AI CodeFix in your IDE"), including the information on how to spot eligible issues in your IDE.

![](https://docs.sonarsource.com/Base64-Image-Removed)

![SonarQube for IntelliJ offers AI CodeFix solutions for taint vulnerabilities found by SonarQube Cloud.](https://assets-eu-01.kc-usercontent.com:443/b1eeb429-d9e0-0100-be87-468f6802040a/5041fc16-5afb-44ce-968c-e810b62d919e/sq-intellij-ai-codefix-taint.png?w=3456&h=2160&auto=format&fit=crop "SonarQube for IntelliJ offers AI CodeFix solutions for taint vulnerabilities found by SonarQube Cloud.")

See the [Fixing issues](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-for-ide/intellij/using/fixing-issues/ "Fixing issues") page for complete details about fixing issues, including [injection and taint vulnerabilities](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-for-ide/intellij/using/fixing-issues/#fixing-taint-vulnerabilities "injection and taint vulnerabilities"), in SonarQube for IntelliJ.
